title: Quiz: Two-Qubit Decomposition
description: Test your understanding of two-qubit decomposition

questions.question: Maximum CNOTs needed for any two-qubit unitary:
questions.explanation: Any two-qubit unitary needs at most 3 CNOTs.

questions.question: SWAP gate decomposes into how many CNOTs?
questions.explanation: SWAP = CX(0,1) @ CX(1,0) @ CX(0,1) requires 3 CNOTs.

questions.question: CZ can be converted to CNOT using only Hadamard gates.
questions.explanation: CZ = H @ CX @ H (Hadamard on target qubit).

questions.question: KAK decomposition expresses two-qubit unitaries as:

options.text: Local unitaries sandwiching an interaction term

questions.explanation: KAK: U = (A1 ⊗ A2) @ K @ (B1 ⊗ B2) with interaction K.
